summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a high‑energy 1980 show with standout jams on Estimated > Wheel > Drums > Space, The Other One and a stellar Bertha solo.
review: The SBD source is praised as clear and crisp—far better than the audience tapes—making every instrument easy to hear. Musically the band is in top form: tight first‑set work, powerful second‑set improvisations, and memorable moments like Estimated > Wheel > Drums > Space, The Other One, a soaring Bertha solo, and an emotionally resonant Wharf Rat.

ai_rating.rationale: All six reviews are 4‑5 stars; reviewers consistently highlight excellent soundboard quality and a superb performance with multiple standout songs.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp SBD with minimal noise; repeatedly called better than any audience (AUD) recordings of the era.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A diverse, tight first set followed by a second‑set that builds into expansive jams; energy stays high throughout and the encore closes with a smoky Good Lovin' and Brokedown Palace.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His tone and improvisational spark are highlighted—he makes up for a botched Stella Blue with an extended jam and delivers a standout Bertha solo.
band_member_comments.Phil: Multiple reviewers credit Phil Lesh’s bass as a key factor in making the show great, noting his deep groove throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Inner interplay between Jerry and Bob (Weir) on Bertha > Greatest Story Told is praised for its tightness and musical conversation.
